meanings.definition: To search for someone or something deliberately and with effort, especially because it is valuable, useful, or important.
examples: Travelers often seek out quiet places away from crowded cities.
She sought out advice from experienced teachers.
Many companies are seeking out skilled programmers.
He always seeks out old bookstores whenever he visits a new town.

meanings.definition: To intentionally try to meet, find, or experience something.
examples: Artists often seek out inspiration from nature.
The journalist sought out people willing to share their stories.
Children naturally seek out adventure and excitement.
She was seeking out opportunities to study abroad.
